title: dendrocolla trichoglottis
intro: Dendrocolla trichoglottis, also known as the "tree moss," is a type of lichenized fungus that grows on trees and branches, combining the characteristics of fungi and algae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dendrocolla trichoglottis include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. In traditional herbal preparations, Dendrocolla trichoglottis is often made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a paste to utilize its medicinal properties.
